proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) is associated with pfs_months; older/higher values may correlate with shorter PFS as is typical for age in oncology.
feature_001 (3-level ordinal, 0/1/2) shows a monotonic relationship with pfs_months, with higher categories associated with shorter PFS (consistent with a performance-status-like variable).
feature_014 (binary; ~55% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months, consistent with an adverse clinical state such as metastatic disease.

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import pearsonr, spearmanr
pearsonr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 shows a strong POSITIVE Pearson correlation with pfs_months (r=0.766, p<1e-300; Spearman r=0.785). Direction is opposite to the typical 'older=worse' age pattern, suggesting feature_015 may be a beneficial composite (e.g., performance/function score) or that PFS in this cohort co-varies with whatever feature_015 measures.

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import f_oneway
f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_001']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_001 level: 5.27 (level 0), 4.05 (level 1), 2.91 (level 2). Monotonic decline, F-test p<1e-300. Each level step decreases PFS by roughly 1.18 months.
Mean pfs_months: 3.70 in feature_014=1 vs 5.06 in feature_014=0 (Welch t-test p<1e-300). Difference -1.35 months. Strong adverse main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features with appreciable prevalence, at least one shows a positive (PFS-extending) association consistent with a beneficial treatment.
feature_006 (binary, ~35% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_023 (binary, ~42% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_031 (binary ~45% prevalence) is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate scan over all binary features identified feature_027 as the only common (~20% prevalence) binary variable with a substantial positive effect on PFS: +0.97 months (4.117 -> 5.089), p<1e-200. This is the leading candidate for a beneficial treatment.
feature_006=1 has mean PFS 4.114 vs 4.419 in feature_006=0. Mean difference -0.305, p=1e-49.
feature_023=1 has mean PFS 4.121 vs 4.448 in feature_023=0. Mean difference -0.327, p=2e-58.
feature_031=1 mean 4.308 vs 4.314; difference -0.006, p=0.77. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (continuous, right-skewed, median 4.4, max 777) is negatively associated with pfs_months consistent with a tumor-burden marker (e.g., elevated CEA/CRP).
feature_019 (continuous, mean 3.8, range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with pfs_months consistent with a beneficial baseline lab (e.g., albumin).
feature_025 (continuous, mean 3.8, range 0-24.6) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.038 (p=3e-17); Spearman r=-0.047 (p=1e-25). Modest but highly significant negative association.
Pearson r=+0.102, p=3e-115. Higher feature_019 -> longer PFS, consistent with albumin-like lab.
Pearson r=-0.123, p=2e-167. Highly significant negative correlation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS regression of pfs_months on all 33 features, feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_027, feature_025, feature_019, feature_023, feature_006, feature_005, feature_022, and feature_021 will all be highly significant independent predictors.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
m = sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(df[features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS (R^2=0.859): feature_015 +0.176/unit (t=453); feature_001 -1.171/level (t=-206); feature_014 -1.362 (t=-176); feature_027 +0.943 (t=+98); feature_025 -0.076/unit (t=-76); feature_019 +0.469/unit (t=+61); feature_023 -0.354 (t=-44); feature_006 -0.287 (t=-36); feature_005 -0.005/unit (t=-21); feature_022 -0.356 (t=-19); feature_021 -0.0004/unit (t=-9). All p<1e-18. Other features non-significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 acts as a beneficial treatment whose efficacy is heterogeneous across patient subgroups; treatment-by-feature interactions will identify modifiers.
feature_006 modifies the feature_027 treatment effect: the PFS benefit of feature_027 is markedly smaller (or absent) in patients with feature_006=1 than in patients with feature_006=0.

analyses.result_summary: TX effect of feature_027: +1.480 months in feature_006=0 vs +0.026 in feature_006=1. Interaction coefficient tx_mod = -1.45, p=4.1e-168. Treatment benefit is essentially eliminated when feature_006=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 modifies the feature_027 treatment effect: PFS benefit is markedly smaller in feature_023=1 than feature_023=0.
feature_022 modifies the feature_027 treatment effect: feature_027 benefit is reversed/eliminated in feature_022=1.

analyses.result_summary: TX effect of feature_027: +1.656 in feature_023=0 vs +0.027 in feature_023=1. tx*feature_023 interaction p=7.7e-226. feature_023=1 abrogates the benefit.
TX effect: +1.027 in feature_022=0 vs -0.156 in feature_022=1 (n=2272). tx*feature_022 p=6.1e-23. feature_022 fully eliminates and slightly reverses benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (rare, 3% prevalence) modifies feature_027 treatment effect: feature_027 yields larger PFS benefit in feature_011=1 than feature_011=0.
feature_005 (continuous) modifies feature_027 treatment effect: lower feature_005 is associated with greater PFS benefit from feature_027.

analyses.result_summary: TX effect: +1.929 in feature_011=1 vs +0.941 in feature_011=0. tx*feature_011 p=1.2e-11. Apparent enhancement of treatment benefit; will revisit later for confounding.
Median-split: TX effect +1.806 below median feature_005, +0.135 above median. tx*feature_005 (linear) p=8.7e-58. Strong negative modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: TX-by-feature interaction screen restricted to features outside {006, 023, 022, 011, 005} will reveal NO additional strong modifiers (i.e., these five capture the heterogeneity).

analyses.code: Loop over remaining 27 features computing TX effect by subgroup and tx*feature interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Among the remaining 27 candidate modifiers, none has |tx*mod| interaction p<1e-3 (best p≈0.10 for feature_007, feature_018, feature_013). feature_015 (age-like): p_int=0.28; feature_001 (PS-like): p_int(linear)=0.89; feature_014 (metastatic-like): p_int=0.15. The five modifiers above (006, 023, 022, 011, 005) capture all detectable heterogeneity in the feature_027 treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining a joint biomarker-favorable subgroup as feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 will dramatically increase the feature_027 treatment effect compared to the unfavorable complement.

analyses.result_summary: Joint subgroup (n=17372): TX effect = +2.758 months, p=0. Complement (n=32628): TX effect = +0.030 months, p=0.29. The three biomarker negatives jointly identify a much larger benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_005 <= 5 to the favorable subgroup definition further enriches feature_027 benefit, with the cleanest dichotomy at feature_005 = 5 (above which the benefit collapses to ~0).

analyses.code: for cut in [4,4.5,5,5.5,6,6.5,7]: split feat_005 within fav3 and compute TX effect
analyses.result_summary: Within fav3, sweeping the feature_005 cutoff: at <=4 TX=+5.04 vs >4 TX=+0.69; at <=5 TX=+5.01 vs >5 TX=-0.002; at <=6 TX=+4.54 vs >6 TX=-0.018. The cleanest knee is at 5.0: TX is essentially zero for feature_005>5 and ~+5 for feature_005<=5.

proposed_hypotheses.text: REFINED: The complete favorable subgroup is feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005 <= 5; within this subgroup feature_027 produces a clinically large and homogeneous PFS benefit (~+5 months), and outside it the benefit is null.

analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup n=9338: TX effect = +5.014 months, p<1e-300. Complement n=40662: TX effect = +0.024 months, p=0.36. Effect ratio essentially 200:1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four predicates is necessary: removing any one degrades the within-subgroup TX effect substantially.

analyses.code: Sensitivity: drop each predicate from the favorable definition and re-estimate TX effect
analyses.result_summary: Drop feature_006 predicate -> n=14492, TX=+3.30; drop feature_023 -> n=16612, TX=+2.85; drop feature_022 -> n=10117, TX=+4.62; drop feature_005<=5 -> n=17372, TX=+2.76. None of the simpler 3-predicate definitions matches the +5.01 of the full 4-predicate definition. All four predicates carry independent information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The apparent feature_011 modifier effect is confounded by the fact that feature_011=1 patients are enriched for the favorable subgroup; once the favorable subgroup is conditioned on, feature_011 no longer modifies the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Within fav subgroup, TX effect by feature_011
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_011=1 patients, fav-subgroup rate = 32.9% vs 18.2% for feature_011=0 (i.e. fav subgroup is over-represented in feature_011=1). Within fav subgroup, TX effect = +5.00 in feature_011=0 (n=8843) vs +5.23 in feature_011=1 (n=495), p_int=0.31. feature_011 is not an independent modifier; its earlier signal was confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, no remaining binary feature significantly modifies the feature_027 effect.

analyses.code: Loop over binary features within fav subgroup; compute TX(mod=1) - TX(mod=0) and tx*mod interaction p
analyses.result_summary: All 13 remaining binary features yield small effect-difference (max |delta| ~0.28 for feature_020) and p_int >= 0.14. No significant residual modification within fav.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, no continuous feature significantly modifies the feature_027 effect.

analyses.code: Loop over continuous features within fav subgroup; median-split TX effect and linear tx*mod interaction p
analyses.result_summary: All 13 continuous features show |TX(hi)-TX(lo)| < 0.30 and tx*mod p >= 0.12 (best feature_032 p=0.12). Within fav, the +5.01 month TX benefit is essentially homogeneous.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding the tx*fav interaction to a multivariable OLS will explain almost all variation in pfs_months and drive the standalone feature_027 main effect to ~0 (i.e., the entire treatment benefit is captured by the favorable subgroup interaction).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ all features + fav + tx_fav
analyses.result_summary: Full adjusted model R^2 jumps from 0.859 (no interaction) to 0.993 (with tx_fav). tx_fav coef = +4.984, t=926, p=0. Standalone feature_027 coef = -0.004 (p=0.08). The tx_fav interaction alone captures essentially all treatment-related signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 assignment is approximately balanced across the favorable vs unfavorable subgroups (i.e., this is not selection of treated patients into favorable status).

analyses.result_summary: Treatment rate: 20.0% overall, 20.4% in favorable subgroup, 20.0% in unfavorable subgroup. Treatment assignment is essentially independent of fav status, so the heterogeneity reflects true biomarker-conditional efficacy, not selection.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-cat) does NOT modify feature_027 treatment effect: TX benefit is similar across feature_001 levels and within each level of feature_001 inside the favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_001 inside and outside fav
analyses.result_summary: Overall TX effect by feature_001 level: +0.92, +0.99, +0.90 (no monotonic pattern). Within fav subgroup: +4.87 (level 0), +5.09 (level 1), +4.81 (level 2). Differences <0.3 months across PS levels. feature_001 is a strong prognostic factor but does NOT modify the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 does NOT modify feature_027 treatment effect (despite being the largest binary prognostic factor).

analyses.result_summary: TX effect: +1.011 in feature_014=1 vs +0.941 in feature_014=0; interaction p=0.15. feature_014 is purely prognostic, not predictive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (the strongest single prognostic factor; r=0.79) does NOT modify feature_027 treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Median-split TX effect: +0.976 (high feature_015) vs +0.956 (low). tx*feature_015 interaction p=0.28. feature_015 is purely prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the rarer binary features (feature_011, 022, 016, 028, 002), only those whose unfavorable level forces patients out of the joint biomarker-favorable subgroup will appear as treatment modifiers.

analyses.code: Compare each rare binary's tx interaction p alone vs adjusted for fav membership
analyses.result_summary: feature_022: prevalence 4.5%, strong tx modifier (p=6e-23) - because feature_022=1 EXCLUDES patient from fav subgroup (a definitional component). feature_011: 3% prevalence, apparent tx modifier (p=1e-11) - REMOVED once fav is conditioned on (p=0.31 within fav). feature_016, 028, 002: prevalence 5/3/0.5%, no tx modification (p > 0.20). Confirms fav definition captures all rare-feature heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When feature_005 is treated as continuous in the unfavorable region, the TX effect remains near zero across the entire range above 5 (i.e., the cutoff is sharp, not a gradient).

analyses.code: Within fav3 (feat006/023/022 all 0), bucket feature_005 above 5 into deciles and compute TX effect
analyses.result_summary: Within fav3, feature_005>5 buckets: TX effect varies between -0.02 and +0.04 across the entire range from 5 to 777. Below 5, TX effect is +5.01 essentially regardless of feature_005 value. Sharp threshold rather than gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other prognostic main effects (feature_015, 001, 014, 019, 025) are stable in magnitude across favorable and unfavorable subgroups, supporting their interpretation as background prognostic factors rather than treatment-related.

analyses.code: Stratified OLS: pfs ~ feature_015+001+014+019+025 within fav==1 and fav==0
analyses.result_summary: Coefficients (fav vs unfav): feature_015 +0.176 vs +0.176; feature_001 -1.16 vs -1.17; feature_014 -1.36 vs -1.37; feature_019 +0.49 vs +0.48; feature_025 -0.078 vs -0.077. Differences tiny (<5%). These five constitute the stable prognostic backbone.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Decision-tree subgroup discovery on TX*fav-style splitting will recover the same favorable subgroup as the manual rule (feat_006=0 & feat_023=0 & feat_022=0 & feat_005<=5).

analyses.code: Iteratively pick the binary/continuous split that maximizes between-subgroup TX-effect variance; greedy 4-step search
analyses.result_summary: Greedy splits selected (in order): feature_023 (0 vs 1) -> feature_006 (0 vs 1) -> feature_005 (<=5 vs >5) -> feature_022 (0 vs 1). The favorable terminal node has TX effect +5.01 (n=9338); other terminal nodes range from -0.16 to +0.13. Mirrors the hand-derived favorable subgroup definition exactly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: feature_027 prolongs progression-free survival by ~+5 months, but ONLY within the subgroup defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005 <= 5. Outside this subgroup the treatment effect is statistically and clinically null. The four predicates are jointly necessary; each one whose unfavorable value is present (feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_022=1, or feature_005>5) suppresses the treatment effect to near zero.

analyses.code: Final tx*fav OLS: pfs ~ tx + fav + tx*fav (and adjusted)
analyses.result_summary: Final model: tx*fav coefficient = +4.99 months (95% CI 4.87-5.11), p<1e-300. Within favorable subgroup (n=9338), feature_027 yields a +5.01 month PFS gain (p<1e-300). Within unfavorable subgroup (n=40662), TX effect = +0.02 months (p=0.36, NS). Adjusted for all 33 features, tx*fav interaction coefficient is +4.98 (t=926); standalone feature_027 effect is -0.004 (NS), confirming all treatment benefit is conditional on the favorable subgroup. Result is clinically large (~doubles PFS in favorable subgroup) and replicable on multiple model specifications.
